name: Moto (AWS Mock Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); no API costs; mock runs locally

auth.notes: Mock intercepts boto3 calls; no real AWS credentials needed in test mode

performance.notes: In-memory mock; no network overhead; fast test execution

agent_readiness.notes: Mock AWS services for Python unit testing. Intercepts boto3 calls and returns mock responses. Supports 90+ AWS services: S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, Lambda, EC2, IAM, Secrets Manager, SSM, Cognito, and many more. Decorator or context manager usage. Server mode for mocking from other languages. pytest integration. No actual AWS account or credentials needed. Essential for fast, deterministic AWS service tests.

gotchas: Not all service behaviors perfectly replicate production AWS
Edge cases and error conditions may differ from real AWS
Server mode adds slight complexity for non-Python tests
Some newer AWS features lag behind real AWS support
best_when: Unit testing Python code that uses AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, SQS, etc.)
CI/CD pipelines that can't use real AWS credentials
Fast, deterministic tests without AWS infrastructure costs
avoid_when: Integration tests requiring real AWS behaviors/consistency
When LocalStack's broader feature set is required
summary: Moto is the standard library for mocking AWS services in Python tests. Intercepts boto3 calls and returns realistic mock responses for 90+ AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, SQS, Lambda, IAM, etc.). No real AWS account needed. Decorator or context manager usage. Fast, deterministic unit tests for AWS-integrated Python code. Essential for CI/CD pipelines that can't use real AWS.
